title: Few-Shot: Show, Don't Just Tell
concept: Including 2-5 worked examples in the prompt to demonstrate the desired input-output mapping.

exercises.prompt: In the previous lesson, we learned about 'zero-shot' prompting. What does zero-shot mean?

options.text: Providing zero examples of the desired output to the AI
Asking the AI to write code with zero bugs
A prompt that has zero words in it

exercises.explain: Zero-shot prompting relies entirely on the model's pre-trained knowledge and instructions, without providing any examples of the expected output.

cards.title: Show, Don't Just Tell!
cards.body: Have you ever tried to explain a complex game to someone, only to find it's much easier to just play a practice round? LLMs work the same way. Showing them a few examples is often much better than writing a giant list of rules.

cards.title: What is Few-Shot Prompting?
cards.body: Few-shot prompting is the technique of inserting 2 to 5 completed examples of inputs and their corresponding outputs directly into your prompt. This helps the AI identify the exact pattern, style, and structure you want.

cards.title: The Recipe Card Analogy
cards.body: Think of few-shot prompting like showing a friend a couple of completed tax forms before asking them to fill out theirs. Instead of writing a long rulebook explaining every detail, you just say: 'Here is what a finished one looks like. Now do this next one.'

cards.title: An Example in Action
cards.body: To classify movie reviews with high precision, you format your prompt like this: 'Review: "Loved it!" -> Sentiment: Positive. Review: "Meh, boring." -> Sentiment: Negative. Review: "It was alright." -> Sentiment: ' The AI will immediately complete the pattern with 'Neutral'.

cards.title: More Isn't Always Better
cards.body: Some developers think you need to provide dozens or hundreds of examples for few-shot prompting to work. In reality, just 2 to 5 high-quality examples are usually more than enough to establish the pattern for the AI.

cards.title: Zero-Shot vs. Few-Shot
cards.body: See how adding clear pairs of examples (few-shot) guides the AI's format much better than simply asking for a result with zero guidance.

exercises.prompt: What is the primary benefit of including examples in a few-shot prompt?

options.text: It reduces the API cost to zero.
It updates the neural network weights permanently.
It shows the model the exact pattern and output format you expect.
It blocks the model from using external web search.

exercises.explain: Providing concrete input-output examples allows the AI to mirror the format, tone, and logic you expect without needing overly complex instructions.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Few-shot prompting permanently updates the AI model's weights to learn the new task.
exercises.explain: Few-shot prompting works dynamically by utilizing the context window, without making any permanent modifications to the AI model's parameters or weights.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing term that describes learning from examples inside a prompt.
exercises.sentence: Providing examples in a prompt lets the model perform in-context learning without any permanent updates.
exercises.explain: When an LLM learns a pattern from examples inside the prompt itself, this is called in-context learning because no weights are updated.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following prompts represents a few-shot prompting approach?

options.text: Can you explain the difference between a fruit and a vegetable in detail?
Translate the following English sentence to French: 'Hello, how are you today?'
Input: Apple -> Output: Fruit. Input: Carrot -> Output: Vegetable. Input: Broccoli -> Output:

exercises.explain: Option C shows a clear, structured pattern of input-output examples followed by an incomplete input, which is the classic structure of few-shot prompting. The other options are zero-shot or conversational.

exercises.prompt: How does few-shot prompting guide an LLM to output the correct format without changing the model's underlying software?

options.text: By updating the weight parameters of the neural network permanently
By establishing a pattern of input-output examples directly in the prompt context
By calling an external database to search for matching answers
By forcing the model to run a python code compiler first

exercises.explain: Few-shot prompting is powerful because it uses in-context learning to show patterns, formatting, and tone directly through input-output pairs, bypassing the need for complex, rule-heavy descriptions that the model might misunderstand.
